preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, here's the thing you need to know about Uriel. And I take a step back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Into him?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No! Away.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's between you and the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, does he hold me if I turn? Well, I just said that, right? Here's the thing about Uriel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you go to move away, his arms that are on your shoulder, tighten, flex, and hold you in place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, you don't want to know? Okay, I'll just save that for later.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Roll for coy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Roll for what, Matt?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Since I don't know what you're going to say, I'm going to say persuasion for this. Because I don't know if it's a deception or not.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is the power out of the DM's hands for a moment?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm going to use my last Luck of the day!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The last one?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Last Luck of the day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, this is persuasion? That's 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That was a big die.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You could give me a-- never mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You want an inspiration?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Huh? No.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) What are you asking for? What are you begging from the DM?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no, that's why I said never mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it begging or is it praying, at this point?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, I'll give inspiration when you've goddamn earned it. As you say that looking at them with this coy look of information about Uriel, they both chuckle in unison. (chuckles) "Shall we?" And she gives a nod and goes, "I think we shall."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I roll for initiative?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The door is currently closed, but you're in eagle form, so you're going to be doing like eagles do. I'll say, you're about there because you were a little bit ahead of them. Those are the two guards right there. At this point now, as this is happening, I need you guys to go ahead and roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I guess running towards the Briarwoods' room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Where you saw them leave? You head up the stairway, following behind where you saw Keyleth and Tiberius rush off to. Are you following the rest of them?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I put my mug down and I start little leg waddling towards them. As I go, I'm going to send a message. I use Message to-- who's the guy who likes us?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: My name is Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, not him. Sylas? No, not Sylas! Seeker Asum?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Seeker Asum, with Message? I believe the person has to be within visual range. Wait, no. You can cast through solid objects if you're familiar with the target and nobody on the barrier. It's 120 foot, though. You don't know how far he is. You could give it a shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I say, something's gone awry. We may need you right away.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The message is delivered out into the space. Hopefully, it found its recipient.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: As I'm running towards the Briarwoods' room, I start shouting Uriel! and grab a servant and say, go find Uriel. Tell him there's enemies within the gates, in the castle. Now!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, Lady Vex. Right away." Runs off. You guys coast up the stairs. Right as you reach the top of the hallway over here, you can see there are two guards that are currently standing at the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I stay at the front door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is the door open?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I'm going to have people who haven't rolled initiative yet, go ahead and roll initiative so I can put you on the timing chart here. Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, he's not in the room. He's in the front yard, at this point. So we are heading to the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait. As that happens, and as my consciousness fades away, I don't say anything, but here's what I think in a split-second. I think of my friends that I have spent so much time with. I think of Keyleth, beautiful, walking under the trees. I think of my twin sister as a young girl, folding up linens with our mother. I think of my sister as an adolescent in lessons, smarter than me. I think of the woman she grew into. Hiking over many, many, many miles together. My best friend. My best friend, Vex'ahlia. And before I can think no more, I say a prayer to Sarenrae to watch over my friends and keep them safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make a wisdom saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With that thought cast out, the darkness swallows your vision and unconsciousness takes you into the cold, wet grass on the floor of the courtyard surrounding the palace. A small shiver runs down your spine, Vex: a feeling you haven't felt before. A feeling of sudden and immediate sourceless dread, and in that instant you know that there is almost no time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I use Hunter's Mark and sense where Lady Briarwood is at that moment?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, she is actually at this point, beyond the palace walls. You can sense and knowing the palace walls. You were expecting a second floor, it's that direction but just outside the walls of the palace. And you guys just got your weapons.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I take off running in that direction.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Hold on. As you've guys are just turning the corner. You can see now Lord Briarwood stands up and is trying to compose himself, ruffles himself up angrily, looking at his damaged circumstance. Lady Briarwood's against his shoulder and she goes, (clicks tongue) "Well, (sighs) at the very least, I think you should come visit us sometime, Percy. You're always welcome back home. It'd be nice of you to visit your family once in a while." Roll initiative. Just you. As you were the first action to take.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I still have my Sleet Storm up. It's a concentration spell. I'm going to move it over there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Can you just shift it over there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. It's a concentration spell. It lasts a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh well no, it's concentration to keep it in the area.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, then I cast it again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you're casting Sleet Storm again. What's the range on it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 150 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 150 feet. The sleet storm lands down. The cobblestone becomes sleek; the carriage begins rocking back and forth. However, it does manage to keep on its course, currently; it does not get knocked prone. It's hard to knock a carriage prone, but it is sliding out of the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is that a disadvantage because it only has three wheels?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Good call, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And it's rough terrain?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That is a two. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, the carriage (crash) slams onto the side of the ground and skids (creaking) to a halt in the middle of the ice, the sleet still spackling down as you guys all turn the corner now; as you approach. The gates have closed (slamming) as the guards have slammed it, listening to Grog on the far side. The door pops open on the side of the carriage. Tiberius, as you've made your way back around and you're at the front now, (panting) and you can see now, about 60 feet in front of you the carriage is overturned in the middle of this sleet storm, on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Where is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From a visual standpoint, you're about 60 to 70 feet from the front of this. The doors slam open and you can see Lord and Lady Briarwood step out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I shoot my exploding arrow?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You are, yes. And we're going to go ahead and have you guys roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're guarded against scrying in your room?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Guards against scrying? Yes, of course!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that true?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Against scrying.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm pretty sure we would have to have an enchantment on our keep that would guard against scrying.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys are having this discussion, a voice rings out behind you and says, "Don't worry. It's not going to help you."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is that little shitty Mr. Missing One Hand?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. You turn around and look, and you can see a gentleman standing there about 25 feet away from you. You see dark brown and black studded leather armor with a cloak over one shoulder. Standing straight, with a grin. Deep dark red skin, burning yellow eyes and jet black, slicked hair between two horns that jut back. A tiefling stands there, looking forward, with two individuals flanking from side to side that are slowly stepping--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what they do, but I'm raising my arm up. Who goes there? Who are you? I've had a really bad day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The tiefling, the male tiefling, steps up forward and says, "I've no quarrel with any of you. We've been scouring this city for a fugitive for a number of weeks. And as luck would have it, you guys happened to smoke her out of her hole."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I go ahead and crack my staff on the ground and cast Anti-life Shell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And are they at the door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys are walking from the outside of the wall to your keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where are they?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right now? They are coming from Emon. You guys are in this in-between section between your keep--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're outdoors? We've left the temple?
